title: ‘Take 5’ when trying out cover crops
content: Set aside 5 acres and give it five years to test out cover crops and decide if they work in your operation.

title: Adapting on the fly: One farmer's approach to a difficult spring
content: Field Snapshot: From deeper planting to fuel monitoring, Ross Frey is adjusting his operation to make the most of less-than-ideal conditions.

title: Why purple fields may trigger more worries than weeds
content: Uncontrolled infestations of purple-hued winter annuals like henbit and purple deadnettle, along with pennycress, may spur reproduction of soybean cyst nematode.

title: Ag Data Privacy Act helps Nebraska farmers protect data
content: Data protection, livestock expansion and brand law were all addressed during this spring’s Nebraska Unicameral session, but no property tax relief made it to the finish line.

title: West River Dairy growth faces scrutiny
content: Riverview LLP’s ambitious plan to expand West River Dairy from 7,855 to 18,855 cows promises economic growth and 40 new jobs, but environmental concerns and community debate are intensifying.

title: Fertilizer crisis is a wake-up call for farmers
content: Sorghum Focus: Geopolitics is squeezing fertilizer supply. Farmers need to build resiliency into their operations today.

title: What farmers should know before signing a data center deal
content: Legal Matters: Energy and AI companies are targeting farmland for development, and not all payments are created equal. Know the difference before you commit.

title: Despite wild weather, early-planted corn starts strong
content: Field Check: Polk County farmer John Samek uses an interesting strategy when planting crops in a region of Missouri with rough soil.

title: Southern Illinois farmers get crops planted and receive timely rain
content: Field Check: Planting for Brandon Barbre, White County, Ill., is off to a great start in 2026, with all his corn in the ground. In the past two weeks, his fields have received some needed rain.

title: Minnesota lost 75% of dairies in 20 years
content: As some dairies expand across Minnesota, farms like the Mosels’ 45-cow operation near Gaylord are fighting for survival. The future of rural communities hangs in the balance.

title: 1969 John Deere 2520 tractor works crops, not just shows
content: Max’s Tractor Shed: Illinois farmer proves classic tractors still earn their keep in modern agriculture.

title: Wheat rockets ahead on tighter supplies
content: Afternoon Market Recap: Corn, soybeans also firm following USDA’s latest WASDE report.

title: Soybeans soar to 2-year highs as USDA signals tighter stocks
content: Wheat hits two-year highs as drought slashes crop to 62-year low; corn tops $5.

title: House set to vote on summer E15
content: Bipartisan bill may finally end temporary waivers and open year-round market for corn growers. Vote expected May 13 after multiple failed attempts in 2024-25.

title: Farm economy struggles as rural Main Street feels the pinch
content: Farm Progress America: Creighton University's Rural Main Street Index hits contraction territory for 13th time since January 2025, signaling widespread economic pain.

title: Big, news-filled week ahead for farmers: Is your game plan ready?
content: Morning Market Analysis: Corn, soybeans follow wheat, crude oil higher overnight ahead of USDA’s Supply and Demand update.

title: China eyes U.S. corn deal ahead of Trump-Xi summit Thursday
content: Beijing in talks to buy American corn, sorghum, DDGs and soybeans during Trump's China visit.

title: Trump delays move to lower tariffs on beef imports
content: Executive orders suspending tariff-rate quotas postponed amid outcry from ranchers and GOP lawmakers. U.S. cattle herd at 75-year low as beef prices hit records.
